sourceMovie.title: Casino
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Casino for the same reason it broke through in 1995: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Martin Scorsese keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) operates in the same crime as Casino, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: GoodFellas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: GoodFellas (1990) belongs to the same conversation as Casino — both anchor their crime in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Once Upon a Time in America
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Casino's crime stayed with you, Once Upon a Time in America (1984) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Donnie Brasco
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Casino excels in crime, Donnie Brasco (1997) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Molly's Game
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Molly's Game (2017) is the natural next step after Casino: same crime DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Carlito's Way
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Casino who respond to its crime will find Carlito's Way (1993) immediately familiar. Both films share a drama sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Lawless
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Casino fans keep landing on Lawless (2012) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: A Walk Among the Tombstones
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Walk Among the Tombstones draws from the same crime well as Casino, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2014 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: A Bronx Tale
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Bronx Tale (1993) captures the crime appeal of Casino without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts A Bronx Tale at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Live by Night
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of Casino — that dark edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Live by Night (2016). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Casino?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, GoodFellas, Once Upon a Time in America—each shares DNA with Casino while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Casino?
faq.answer: Casino (1995) is best described as Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Casino worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
